subject: Science
chapter: Our Home: Earth, a Unique Life Sustaining Planet
source_pdf: Our Home Earth A Unique Life Sustaining Planet.pdf

activities_projects.title: Designing a Model Habitable Planet
activities_projects.objective: Understand the factors required for a planet to support life
steps: Study conditions required for life such as atmosphere, temperature, water, and gravity
Design a hypothetical planet that could support life
Specify its distance from the star, atmosphere composition, and temperature range
Present the model and justify why it could support life
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students integrate scientific concepts to design a life-supporting planet
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific reasoning and design explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mars Habitat Planning Project
activities_projects.objective: Explore challenges of sustaining human life on Mars
steps: Research Mars conditions such as temperature, atmosphere, and gravity
Identify challenges humans would face on Mars
Design a settlement that provides oxygen, water, food, and protection from radiation
Present a detailed survival plan
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand planetary habitability and technological solutions
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of plan and scientific accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Modeling the Carbon Cycle
activities_projects.objective: Understand the movement of carbon between Earth’s systems
steps: Research the carbon cycle processes
Create a diagram showing carbon movement between atmosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere, and geosphere
Explain the role of plants, oceans, and fossil fuels
Discuss how human activities affect the carbon cycle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze interactions among Earth systems
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of carbon cycle explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Climate Change Impact Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how climate change affects ecosystems
steps: Collect temperature and rainfall data for your region
Plot graphs showing trends
Analyze possible changes in plant or animal populations
Suggest measures to reduce climate change effects
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret environmental data and propose solutions

activities_projects.title: Atmosphere Protection Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the ozone layer protects life
steps: Shine UV light on plain paper
Place sunscreen or transparent sheet between the light and paper
Observe the change in intensity
Relate the results to ozone layer protection
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how atmospheric layers protect living organisms

activities_projects.title: Ecosystem Interdependence Study
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how organisms depend on each other
steps: Select an ecosystem such as forest, pond, or grassland
List producers, consumers, and decomposers
Create a food web diagram
Explain what would happen if one species disappeared
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand ecological balance and biodiversity
activities_projects.evaluation: Food web accuracy and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Magnetic Field Shield Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate how magnetic fields deflect charged particles
steps: Observe magnetic field patterns using iron filings
Use a compass to track field direction
Discuss how Earth's magnetic field deflects solar wind
Relate the model to planetary protection
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the protective function of Earth's magnetic field

activities_projects.title: Reproduction Strategy Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Analyze advantages and disadvantages of different reproductive strategies
steps: Study examples of asexual and sexual reproduction
Compare characteristics such as genetic variation and survival rate
Discuss examples like plants, fish, birds, and mammals
Prepare a comparative report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand evolutionary advantages of reproduction types

activities_projects.title: Local Pollution Survey
activities_projects.objective: Investigate pollution sources in the local environment
steps: Identify sources of air, water, or soil pollution in your area
Collect evidence through observation or photographs
Interview local residents about environmental issues
Prepare a report with suggested solutions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop environmental awareness and research skills

activities_projects.title: Earth System Interaction Model
activities_projects.objective: Understand interactions among atmosphere, hydrosphere, biosphere, and geosphere
steps: Draw a model showing the four Earth systems
Show how energy and matter move between them
Explain examples such as rainfall, plant growth, and soil formation
Discuss how human actions disturb these interactions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyze complex Earth system interactions
